item.title: Revolut Gets UK Approval to Expand Investments for Customers
item.description: Revolut Ltd. has received approval from UK regulators to expand its investment offerings in the country, as the fintech firm seeks to win more wealthy clients.

item.title: Myanmar Junta’s Forced Remittance Rules Pull in $5.6 Billion
item.description: Myanmar has turned migrant remittances into its biggest source of foreign inflows, finance ministry data seen by Bloomberg News show, after the military imposed rules that pressure citizens abroad to send money home.

item.title: India Faces Challenge of Bridging BRICS Rift Over Iran War
item.description: India faces the challenge of bridging deep divisions within the BRICS bloc gathering in New Delhi this week, as members remain split over the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Adtek Is Said to Explore Hong Kong IPO at $4 Billion Valuation
item.description: Shenzhen Adtek Technology Co. is considering an initial public offering in Hong Kong, according to people familiar with the matter, adding to a wave of Chinese listings by businesses related to data centers and artificial intelligence.

item.title: India Considers Reducing Taxes on Bond Investments by Foreigners
item.description: India is considering a significant reduction in the taxes paid by foreign investors on the nation’s bonds as authorities seek to align policies with global norms and attract inflows, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Nvidia Partner Hon Hai Profit Jumps After AI Fuels Server Sales
item.description: Nvidia Corp.’s major server assembly partner Hon Hai Precision Industry Co. reported a stronger-than-expected increase in quarterly profit, highlighting sustained spending on hardware essential for AI.

item.title: Burberry’s Turnaround Boosted by Strong Demand for Scarves
item.description: Burberry Group Plc sales rose more than expected at the end of the fiscal year, indicating the turnaround under Chief Executive Joshua Schulman is paying off with a focus on its signature scarves and trench coats.

item.title: UK Posts Strongest Growth in a Year But Headwinds Are Building
item.description: The UK kicked off the year with a burst of economic growth, with business and consumers appearing to hold up in the initial weeks of the Iran war.

item.title: Manipal Hospitals Said to Start Marketing $1 Billion IPO Soon
item.description: Manipal Health Enterprises Pvt., which runs the Manipal Hospitals chain, is set to begin formal marketing as early as next week for its planned initial public offering to raise more than $1 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Two India-Bound LPG Carriers Add to Uptick in Hormuz Transits
item.description: Two India-bound vessels laden with cooking fuel from the Persian Gulf appear to have transited the Strait of Hormuz, making them the latest to exit despite continued restrictions from the US and Iran.

item.title: London Office Leasing Rebounds on Demand Fueled by Finance Firms
item.description: London office leasing bounced back from Covid-era lows, with tenant floor-space expansions reaching a six-year high on rising demand from finance and professional services firms, according to broker Cushman & Wakefield.

item.title: Abu Dhabi’s L’imad Eyes $30 Billion Infra Push With Temasek, GIP
item.description: Abu Dhabi’s L’imad Holding is joining local and global partners in an investment venture targeting $30 billion of infrastructure projects, underscoring the central role the new sovereign wealth fund is likely to play in the emirate’s ambitions.

item.title: UK Economy Unexpectedly Grew in March Despite Iran War Impact
Telefónica Quarterly Earnings Rise as Spanish Competition Eases
item.description: Telefónica SA reported a slight increase in first-quarter earnings profit, boosted by operations in Brazil and easing competition in Spain.

item.title: Chinese Stocks Fall as Markets Await Signals From Xi-Trump Talks
item.description: Chinese equities slipped as President Xi Jinping met with his US counterpart Donald Trump, with investors taking some profits after a recent tech-driven rally while waiting for clearer signals from the talks.

item.title: India Asks US for Russian Waiver Extension as Iran War Drags On
item.description: India has asked the US to extend its waiver on Russian oil, according to people familiar with the matter, as the nearly 11-week war in the Persian Gulf disrupts energy supply.

item.title: ECB to Hike If Oil Deanchors Inflation Expectations, Kazaks Says
item.description: The European Central Bank will need to raise borrowing costs if an increase in crude prices feeds through to inflation expectations, according to Governing Council member Martins Kazaks.

item.title: Space Stock Craze Comes to Europe as One British Fund Quadruples
item.description: It’s not just US retail investors piling into any asset connected to space exploration. Europeans want in, too.

item.title: Australia Steel-Making Coal Recovery Stalls, Data Firm Says
item.description: A number of Australian steel-making coal mines may have struggled to sustain recent recoveries in output, potentially supporting prices of the commodity, according to a satellite-data analytics firm.

item.title: Seoul Apartment Price Gains Pick Up Pace, Adding Pressure on BOK
item.description: A housing rally in South Korea’s capital gained momentum, reinforcing the case for the nation’s central bank to shift to a hawkish tilt later this month as it grapples with mounting inflationary pressures from the Iran conflict.

item.title: Polish Economic Risks Top Inflation Concerns for MPC’s Wnorowski
item.description: Poland’s economic growth may face a bigger jolt from the Middle East conflict than the country’s inflation, according to Monetary Policy Council member Henryk Wnorowski.

item.title: BOJ’s Masu Says Rate Hike Desirable at ‘Earliest Stage Possible’
item.description: A Bank of Japan board member called for interest rates to be increased as soon as possible provided there is no indication of the economy running into trouble, citing more enduring inflationary risks from the war in Iran.

item.title: Why the ECB’s June Interest-Rate Hike Is Becoming Less Certain
item.description: Two weeks after Christine Lagarde put investors on alert that the European Central Bank is moving toward raising interest rates, a hike in June is becoming less obvious.

item.title: Japan’s Eneos Buys Chevron Asia Oil Assets for $2.2 Billion
item.description: Japanese refiner Eneos Holdings Inc. agreed to purchase some of Chevron Corp.’s refining and retail assets across the Asia-Pacific for $2.17 billion, significantly expanding its footprint across the region.

item.title: Europe Plays Catch-Up in Africa as Emerging Powers Boost Investment
item.description: A summit in Nairobi yielded €14 billion in French investment commitments, but Europeans have fallen far behind newer arrivals in the region.

item.title: Is Polaroid Coming for Today's Stock Market?
item.description: Market concentration in the AI Boom is even worse than in the Nifty Fifty era.

item.title: Global Funds Speed Up Korea Stock Exit as Record Rally Extends
item.description: Foreign investors have deepened their retreat from South Korean stocks, even as the market has kept climbing to new highs on local inflows and the artificial intelligence boom.

item.title: Japan’s 30-Year Bond Sale Draws Firmer Demand on Higher Yields
item.description: Japan’s 30-year government bond auction Thursday saw slightly stronger demand than the 12-month average as higher yields underpinned buying.

item.title: Copper Retreats From Record Close as Purchases in China Slow
item.description: Copper retreated from a record-high close as the rally started to deter purchases in China, where investors were monitoring the summit between Chinese leader Xi Jinping and US President Donald Trump.

item.title: What India’s Top Three Mutual Funds Bought and Sold in April
item.description: Indian investors plowed money into equity funds for a 62nd straight month in April, though inflows eased from the previous month.
